report_name: Toy Giant Hasbro Hit by Cyberattack, Recovery to Take Weeks

threat_actors.name: Unnamed Threat Actor responsible for Hasbro breach
threat_actors.description: A sophisticated, unnamed threat actor that conducted a cyberattack against Hasbro in March 2026, causing significant operational disruption and requiring a multi-week recovery period. The actor established persistence and caused a material impact on the company's operations.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The attacker used 'wmic.exe' to delete volume shadow copies, a common technique to prevent system recovery and a precursor to ransomware deployment.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The cyberattack caused significant operational disruption, forcing Hasbro to shut down systems and rely on manual processes for weeks. This level of impact is consistent with a ransomware attack where data is encrypted to disrupt business operations.

tools.name: wmic
tools.description: The Windows Management Instrumentation Command-line utility was used to delete volume shadow copies to inhibit system recovery.

identities.name: Hasbro, Inc.
identities.description: The victim of a significant cyberattack in March 2026 that caused major disruptions to its business operations.

indicators.name: WMIC Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: WMIC Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 2b8a1b9c-7b4e-4c8d-8f2a-9e7b1c3d4e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of wmic.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common technique used by ransomware to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://mlq.ai/cybersecurity-tech/hasbro-confirms-cyberattack-with-weeks-long-recovery-expected/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-02
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\wmic.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'shadowcopy'
      - 'delete'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of the wmic.exe utility to delete volume shadow copies, a technique often used by ransomware before encryption.
